task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo web interface (dashboard or app menu) is visible in a web browser.

task_completion.description: Locate and open the specific expense report for Sarah Mitchell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo Expenses app is open, displaying the expense report titled 'Q1 Client Visit - Chicago' for employee 'Sarah Mitchell'.
The status pipeline/breadcrumb at the top right of the expense report shows it is in the 'Approved' (or 'approve') state.
The status pipeline/breadcrumb shows the report is in the 'Posted' (or 'post') state, and/or a 'Journal Entry' smart button is visible.
The status pipeline/breadcrumb shows the report is in the 'Done' state, or an 'In Payment' / 'Paid' ribbon is visible on the record.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to interact with Odoo.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window with the Odoo interface is visible. The agent did not use terminal commands or python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Odoo application rather than a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard Odoo interface elements (menus, breadcrumbs, URL bar) are present, not a text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the status.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Elements/Console tab) are open to manipulate the DOM and fake the 'Done' status.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Task: employee_expense_approval. Employee: Sarah Mitchell. Expense Report: 'Q1 Client Visit - Chicago'. Total Amount: $1,117.50. The expected Odoo state progression for the expense report is 'submit' -> 'approve' -> 'post' -> 'done'.
